key_insight: In RLVR, orthonormal LoRA initialization best preserves the geometry of full fine-tuning, reducing the gap to dense training and improving stability.
engineering_relevance: For parameter-efficient fine-tuning in math-reasoning RL pipelines, choosing the right low-rank initialization can materially affect convergence, robustness, and final performance.
method: Analyze LoRA dynamics under RLVR and compare initialization schemes to full fine-tuning.
Derive that orthonormal initialization minimizes the outcome gap to full fine-tuning.
Propose geometry-preserving variants RLPO and RLMO based on orthonormal initialization.
evidence_signal: Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks show the proposed initialization stabilizes RLVR training and outperforms standard LoRA, while PiSSA and MiLoRA can underperform.
watch_item: The results are benchmark-dependent and the benefit may rely on correctly implementing orthonormal initialization within the specific RLVR training setup.
paper_title: Geometry-Preserving Orthonormal Initialization for Low-Rank Adaptation in RLVR
